text: CASTEAU, a commune of Belgium, in the dep. of Hainaut, 6 m. N of Mons. Pop. 1,492.
CASTEGGIO, a town of the Sardinian states, in the prov. and 6 m. E of Voghera. Pop. 2,200.

CASTEJON, a town of Spain, in New Castile, in the prov. of Guadalaxara, 8 m. SW of Siguenza, on an affluent of the Henares .- Also a town of Navarre, 6 m. WNW of Tudela, on the r. bank of the Ebro.—Also a village in the prov. and 8 m. NW of Cuenca.
CASTEJON-DE-MONEGRO, a town of Spain, in the prov. and 7 m. SSE of Huesca, at the foot of the Alcubiere mountain.
CASTEL, a town of Bavaria, in the circle of Regen or the Upper Pfalz, on the N slope of the Steigerwald, 9 m. SW of Amberg. Pop. 812.
CASTEL-A-MARE. See CASTELLAMARE.
CASTEL-ARQUATO, a town in the duchy of Parma, 12 m. W of Borgo-San-Donnino.
CASTELBAJAC, a commune of France, in the dep. of Hautes-Pyrenees, cant. of Galan. Pop. 781.
CASTEL-BALDO, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, deleg. and 30 m. SW of Padua, on the l. bank of the Adige, in N lat. 45° 6′ 57". Pop. 2,000.
CASTEL-BELFORTE, a village of Lombardy, in the deleg. and 6 m. NE of Mantua.
CASTEL-BOLOGNESE, a town in the Statesof-the-Church, in the leg. and 20 m. WSW of Ravenna, near the l. bank of the Senio.
CASTELBRIANTE. See CHATEAUBRIANT.
CASTELBUONO, a canton and village of Sicily, in the district and 8 m. SSE of Cefalu. Pop. 7,000.
CASTELCAO, a village of Portugal, in the prov. of Beira, comarca and 9 m. NNE of Trancoso.
CASTEL-CAMBIO, a village of Austria, in Dalmatia, in the circle and on the gulf of Spalatro.
CASTELCICALA, a village of Naples, in the Terra-di-Lavora, 1 m. E of Nola. Pop. 180.
CASTEL-CUCCO, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, deleg. and 21 m. WNW of Treviso. Pop. 1,400. Woollen stuffs are here manufactured.
CASTELCULIER, a town of France, in the dep. of Lot-et-Garonne, cant. of Puymirol. Pop. 824.
CASTEL-D'AGOGNA, a town of the Sardinian states, in the prov. of Lomellina, 2 m. SW of Mortara, near the l. bank of the Agogna. Pop. 600.
CASTEL-DE'-FRANCHI, a town of Naples, in Principato-Ultra, 6 m. W of San-Angelo-Lombardi, on the r. bank of the Calore. Pop. 2,100.
CASTEL-DELLA-PIETRA, a village of Austria, in the Tyrol, circle and 4 m. NE of Reveredo, on the 1. bank of the Adige.—The Venetians were here defeated by the archduke Sigismond in 1487.
CASTEL-DELPHINO, or CHATEAU-DAUPHIN, a town of the Sardinian states, in the prov. and 20 m. WSW of Saluces, near the source of the Vraita, in a defile of Mont-Viso, in the Maritime Alps.
CASTEL-DEL-PIANO, a town of Tuscany, in the comp. and 25 m. NE of Grosseto, on Monte Amiata. Pop. 2,359.
CASTEL-DEL-RIO, a town in the States-of-the- Church, leg. of Ravenna, 20 m. SSE of Bologna, on the l. bank of the Santerno.
CASTEL-DI-SANGRO, a town of Naples, in Abruzzo-Ultra 2da, district and 18 m. SSE of Salmona, on a mountain at the foot of which flows the Sangro. Pop. 3,090.

CASTELET, a hamlet of France, in the dep. of Ariege, com. of Perles, 19 m. SE of Foix. Pop. 500. There are iron forges here. CARLED STATE OF THE POST PARTICIPATE ARCHITICAL STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE STATE
CASTELET (LE), a commune and village of France, in the dep. of Var, cant. of Le Beausset, 8 m. NW of Toulon. Pop. 1,946.
CASTEL-FABI, a village of Spain, in the prov. of Teruel, in Valencia, 20 m. SSW of Ternel, on an affluent of the Guadalaviar.
CASTEL-FIORENTINO, a town of Tuscany, in the comp. and 18 m. NE of Florence, on the Elsa. Pop. 2,630. Important fairs are held here.
CASTEL-FOCOGNANO, a parish and village of Tuscany, in the comp. and 15 m. NW of Arezzo. Pop. 268.
CASTEL-FOLLIT, a town of Spain, in Catalonia, in the prov. and 18 m. NW of Girone, on the Fluvia. This was at one time a fortified place.
CASTELFRANC, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of Lot, cant. of Luzech, 10 m. WNW of Cahors. Pop. 775.

CASTEL-FRANCO, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, deleg. and 18 m. W of Treviso, on the r. bank of the Musone, at an alt. of 432 ft. above sea-level. Pop. 4,000. It is fortified, and surrounded by old walls and ramparts.—Also a town of Naples, in the prov. of Capitanata, 14 m. WNW of Bovino. Pop. 3,092.—Also a town in the States-of-the-Church, leg. and 15 m. WNW of Bologna.
CASTEL-FRANCO-DI-SOPRA, a parish and village of Tuscany, in the comp. and 20 m. W of N from Arezzo, near the Arno, at an alt. of 951 ft. above sea-level. Pop. 1,184.

CASTEL-FRANCO-DI-SOTTO, a town of Tuscany, in the comp. and 26 m. W of Florence, on the r. bank of the Arno, at an alt. of 59 ft. above sealevel. Pop. 3,077. It contains manufactories of pottery and linen.

CASTEL-GANDOLFO, a village of the Statesof-the-Church, in the comarca and 12 m. SE of Rome, beautifully situated near the W bank of Lake Albano, at an alt. of 1,332 ft. above sea-level. It contains a residence belonging to the sovereign pontiffs, and some ancient remains, including those of the palace of Domitian.
CASTEL-GENOVESE. See CASTEL-SARDO.

CASTEL-GOFFREDO, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, delegation and 20 m. NW of Mantua. Pop. 3,200. It is defended by old walls; and contains an hospital and two silkspinning-mills. A fair is held once a-year.
CASTEL-GOMBERTO, a village of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, prov. and 9 m. NW of Vicenza. Pop. 2,300. A fair is held once a-year.
CASTEL-GRANDE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Basilicata, district and 18 m. SW of Melfi. Pop. 3,363. It has several important fairs.
CASTEL-GUELFO, a town of the duchy of Parma, in the district and 9 m. ESE of Borgo-San- Donnino, on the l. bank of the Taro. It has a castle.

CASTEL-GUGLIELMO, a village of Venetian Lombardy, in the prov. of Polesina, district and 6 m. SSW of Lendinara, and 14 m. WSW of Rovigo, on the r. bank of the Bianco canal. Pop. 1,960. It possesses an old castle, and has a weekly market.
CASTELHANO, a river of Brazil, in the prov. of São Paulo, comarca of Curitiba.

CASTELHANOS, a town of Brazil, in the prov. of Ceara, district of Januaria, and parish of Acaracu, on the coast between the Jericoacoara and Acaracu.—Also an extensive bay of the prov. of São Pedro-do-Rio-Grande. It is sheltered from the E and SE winds, and is capable of receiving vessels of the largest size. To the S of its entrance, in S lat. 34° 20', is a dark and jagged reef named Castilhos- Grandes, which marks the boundary between Brazil and Monte-Video. * CAS

CASTEL-JALOUX, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Garonne, arrond. of Nerac. The cant. comprises 10 com. Pop. in 1831, 6,279; in 1841, 6,552. The town is pleasantly situated in a fertile district on the Avance, 20 m. NW of Nerac, and 30 m. WNW of Agene, near the road from Toulouse to Bordeaux. It possesses manufactories of woollen fabrics, glass-works, papermills, tanneries, forges, &c., and has an active trade in cattle, wines, grain, chestnuts, wax, honey, tan, cork, pitch, rosin, and leeches. Fairs are held twice a-year. In the vicinity are the ruins of the castle of the lords of Albret.
CASTELLA. See CASTILLA. CASTELLABATE, CASTEL - DELL'ABATE, or

ABATE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Principato-Citra, district and 20 m. WNW of Il Vallo, near the Mediterranean. Pop. 2,800. Cotton and wine are cultivated in the environs, and in the vicinity are productive fisheries.

CASTELLAMARE, or Castel-a-Mare, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Abruzzo - Ultra, district and 15 m. E of Civita-di-Penne, on a rising ground, near the Adriatic, in N lat. 42° 28′ 54". Pop. 3,961.— Also a town and port, cap. of a district of the same name, in the prov. and 18 m. SE of Naples, finely situated on the gulf of that name. Pop. 18,452. It is defended by two forts, and contains a cathedral, 5 churches, several convents, a royal residence, a military hospital, excellent barracks, a building-dock with extensive magazines, thermal and mineral baths, &c. Silk, cotton and linen fabrics, and leather, form the chief articles of local manufacture. This town occupies the site of the ancient Stabiæ, which, in 79 was destroyed by an eruption of Vesuvius. The dist. of C. comprises 10 cant., and is generally fertile. - Also a town of Sicily, in the prov. and 20 m. E of Trapani, district and 6 m. NW of Alcamo, on a gulf of the same name. Pop. 8,034. It has a small port and a custom-house, and possesses a considerable trade in wheat, wine, fruit, oil, anchovies, and lint. See also PALERMO.
CASTELLAMARE-DELLA-BRUCA, or Cas-

TEL-A-MARE-DELLA-BRUCA, a village of Naples, in the prov. of Principato-Citra, district and 8 m. SW of Il Vallo, near the Mediterranean. It occupies the site of the ancient Helia or Velia. The environs afford excellent manna.

CASTELLAMONTE, a town of Piedmont, in the prov. and 10 m. SW of Ivrea, on a hill, near the l. bank of the Orco. Pop. 5,200. It contains a castle and a large market-place, and has extensive manufactories of pottery. Its trade consists chiefly in cattle, butter, cheese, and wine.
CASTELLANA, a town of Naples, in the prov. of the Terra-di-Bari, district and 28 m. SE of Bari. Pop. 7,176.

CASTELLANETA, a town of Naples, in the prov. of the Terra-d'Otranto, district and 20 m. NW of Tarento, on the Gravina-di-Castellanate. Pop. 5,453. It contains a cathedral, 5 convents, an hospital, and several charitable establishments. The circondario produces considerable quantities of cotton.

CASTELLANNE, an arrondissement, canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Basses-Alpes. The arrond. comprises an area of 132,848 hectares, and six cant., viz.: Saint-Andre, Annot, Castellanne, Colmars, Entrevaux, and Senez. Pop. in 1831, 23,101; in 1841, 23,770.—The cant. contains 13 com. Pop. in 1831, 5,292; in 1841, 5,925.—The town, the ancient Salinoe, is situated in a fertile valley, on the r. bank of the Verdon, 23 m. SE of Digne. Pop. in 1789, 1638; in 1821, 1,930; in 1831, 2,106; in 1836, 2,069; and in 1841, 2,252. It contains a communal college, and has manufactories of common cloth. The trade consists chiefly in comfits and dried fruits, especially plums. In the vicinity is a saline spring from which the original name of the town was derived.
CASTELLANOS, a town of Spain, in the prov. and 22 m. ESE of Leon, partido of Sahagun, on an affluent of the Cea.
CASTELLANOS-DE-CASTRO, a town of Spain, in Old Castile, in the prov. and 17 m. W of Burgos, partido and 6 m. E of Castrojerez, near the r. bank of the Garbanzuelo.
CASTELLAR, a town of Spain, in Andalusia, in the prov. and 50 m. ESE of Cadiz, partido and 8 m. NW of St. Roque, near the l. bank of the Guadarranque.
CASTELLARO-LAGUSELLO, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, delegation and 20 m. NNW of Mantua. Pop. 1,000. A fair is held here once a-year.

CASTELLAUN, or KASTELLAUN, a town of Prussia, in the prov. of the Rhine, regency and 20 m. S of Coblenz, circle and 7 m. NW of Simmern. Pop. 1,350. It contains a castle, much dilapidated, and a Catholic and a Lutheran church.

CASTELLAZZO, a town of Piedmont, in the prov. and 4 m. S of Alexandria, between the Bormida and Orba. Pop. 5,000. It contains 4 churches, and 5 convents. The trade consists in articles of local produce.
CASTELLE, a village of Naples, in the prov. of Calabria-Ultra, district and 16 m. SSW of Cotrone, on the gulf of Squillace. Pop. 316. It possesses a small port, and a custom-house.

CASTELLEONE, formerly Castel-Manfredi, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, prov. and 17 m. NW of Cremona, district and 5 m. W of Soresina. Pop. 4,300. It is surrounded with old walls, and is regularly built. A highly esteemed species of mustard is manufactured here.
CASTELLETTO-MASCAGNI, a village of Tuscany, in the comp. of Sienna, 3 m. N of Chiusdino. Pop. 375.
CASTELLETTO-D'ORBA, a town of Piedmont, in the prov. and 9 m. SSW of Novi. Pop. 1,000.
CASTELLETTO-SOPRA-TICINO, a town of Piedmont, in the prov. and 24 m. N of Novara, near the r. bank of the Tessino. Pop. 3,200. It contains a church, and a convent.
CASTELLFORT, a town of Spain, in Valencia, in the prov. and 39 m. NNW of Castellon-de-la- Plana, and partido of Morella. In the vicinity is a mine of iron.
CASTELLINA-IN-CHIANTE, a parish of Tuscany, in the comp. and 13 m. N of Sienna, at an alt. of 1,903 ft. above sea-level. Pop. 688. The district produces excellent wine.
CASTELLINA-MARITTIMA, a parish of Tuscany, in the prov. and 37 m. SE of Pisa. Pop. 1,284. It contains the richest alabaster mines in the duchy.
CASTELLNOU, a town of Spain, in Valencia, in the prov. and 20 m. WSW of Castellon-de-la- Plana, partido and 4 m. E of Segorbe, near the 1. bank of the Canales. Pop. 1,500.

CASTELLO, a village of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Venice, delegation and 15 m. NNE of Belluno, on the r. bank of the Piave. Pop. 1,100. It contains a school; and has an active commerce in iron, copper, and silk.

CASTELLO-BARONIA, a village of Naples, in the prov. of Principato-Ultra, district and 12 m. SE of Ariano. Pop. 2,300. It contains a castle, and 2 churches, and has some manufactories of woollen fabrics. Mineral springs exist here.

CASTELLO-BRANCO, a town of Portugal, cap. of Lower Beira, in the prov. of Beira, 60 m. ESE of Pop. 500. CAS Coimbra, on the Vereza or Leca. Pop. 6,000. It is surrounded by a double wall, flanked with 7 towers, and defended by a good citadel; and contains 2 churches, 2 convents, 2 hospitals, an alms-house, a college, and an Episcopal palace.

CASTELLO-DE-VIDE, a town of Portugal, in the prov. of Alentejo, comarca and 9 m. N of Portalegre. Pop. 6,000. It stands on a hill, and is defended by an old wall and a castle. It contains 3 churches, and has a cloth manufactory.
CASTELLO-DI-QUARTO, a parish and village of Tuscany, in the comp. and 4 m. N of Florence. Pop. 1,346. It contains a villa belonging to the grand-duke of Tuscany.
CASTELLO-MELHOR, a town of Portugal, in the prov. of Beira, 40 m. ESE of Lamego, on the l. bank of the Douro.
CASTELLO-MENDO, a town of Portugal, in the prov. of Beira, comarca and 25 m. SE of Trancoso. It contains a fortress, 3 churches, an hospital, and an alms-house.

CASTELLON (SIERRA DE), a mountain of Spain, a ramification of the mountains of Toledo, in New Castile, in the prov. of Ciudad-Real. It runs SW between the Rubial and Valdehornos, and terminates on the Guadiana.
CASTELLON-DE-AMPURIAS, a town of Spain, in Catalonia, in the prov. and 28 m. NE of Gerona, partido of Figueras, on the Muga, 3 m. above its entrance into the gulf of Rosas. Pop. 2,706.

CASTELLON-DE-LA-PLANA, a province, judicial partido, and town of Spain, in the kingdom of Valencia. The prov. is bounded on the NE by that of Tarragona; on the SE by the Mediterranean; on the SW by the prov. of Valencia; and on the NW by that of Teruel; comprising a superficies of 198 Spanish sq. leagues; and a pop. in 1834 of 199,220. It contains 10 judicial partidos, viz.: Albocacer, C., Lucena, Morella, Nules, San-Mateo, Segorbe, Villa- Real, Vinaroz, and Vivel; and 154 pueblos or communes. It is generally mountainous; and is watered by the Cenia on the N; and by the Servol, Villahermosa, Mijares, &c. The chief productions of the soil are fruit, wheat, maize, silk, wine, oil, onions, &c.—The principal line of road is that from Valencia to Barcelona, by Tarragona.—The partido of C. comprises 8 pueblos.—The town is situated in a wide and fertile plain, near the l. bank of the Monleon, 3 m. above the confluence of that river with the Mijares, 43 m. NNE of Valencia, and 195 m. E of Madrid, on the road from Valencia to Barcelona. Pop. 16,952. It has two suburbs, and is generally well-built. Its streets are spacious and straight; and it possesses 2 large public squares, one of which contains the townhouse and the principal church, and the other, several fine alleys of trees. Sail-cloth forms the chief article of local manufacture.—This town was built by James I., king of Aragon, after the destruction in 1233 of the ancient Castalia, the ruins of which are still to be found on a rising ground in the vicinity.

CASTELLONE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of the Terra - di - Lavora, district and 5 m. NE of Gaeta, and on the gulf of that name. Pop. 3,940. It contains a church and two convents. In the environs is an ancient structure, called the tower of Cicero, which is supposed to mark the spot where he was killed. In 1663 the Lombards were defeated here by Constant II.—Also a village of the same prov., in the district and 30 m. NW of Piedimonte, on the l. bank and near the source of the Volturno. Pop. 672.
CASTELLO-NOVO, a town of Portugal, in the prov. of Beira, comarca and 19 m. NNE of Castello- Branco. In the vicinity are the ruins of a fortress. ~~ ~
CASTELLO-REALE. See MAGAZAN.

CASTELLO-RODRIGO, a town of Portugal, in the prov. of Beira, comarca of Trancoso, 15 m. NNE of Pinhel. Pop. 300. It is surrounded by walls, flanked with towers, now greatly dilapidated; and is defended by a citadel. It contains a castle, a parishchurch, and an alms-house.
CASTELLO-SAN-CATALDO, a town of Naples, in the prov. of the Terra-d'Otranto, district and 10 m. ENE of Lecce, on the Adriatic.
CASTELLOTE-Y-SU-BARRIO-MONTIJO, a town of Spain, in Aragon, cap. of a judicial partido, in the prov. and 55 m. NE of Teruel, on the l. bank of the Guadalupe. Pop. 2,475.

CASTELLOUBON, a valley of France, in the dep. of the Hautes-Pyrenees, cant. of Lourdes. It is traversed in all its extent by the Louey and Nos, torrents which descend from the peak of Montaigu, the highest summit of the chain which separates this valley from that of Bareges. It contains several mineral springs, and a fine species of white slate, and encloses 16 villages.

CASTELLUCCIA, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Principato-Citra, district and 15 m. SSE of Campagna, and 29 m. SE of Salerno, on a hill about a mile from the Calore, over which is a fine stonebridge. Pop. 1,328. It contains 2 handsome churches and 3 convents.
CASTELLUCCIA - DI - VAL - MAGGIORE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Capitanata, district and 11 m. NW of Bovino, on the Celone. Pop. 1,600.

CASTELLUCCIO, a town of Naples, in the prov. of the Terra-di-Lavora, district and 8 m. SW of Sora. Pop. 1,229. It contains a custom-house.— Also a village of the States-of-the-Church, in the delegation of Spoleto, 5 m. E of Norcia, on the S side of Monte Sibella, at an alt. of 4,763 ft. above sealevel. It is the most elevated village in the Apennines,—Also a village of Venetian Lombardy, in the prov. and 8 m. W of Mantua, district and 7 m. NE of Marcaria, on the Osone. Pop. 2,000.
CASTELLUCCIO-INFERIORE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Basilicata, district and 18 m. SE of Lago-Negro, and 51 m. S of Potenza, in a plain. Pop. 2,908.
CASTELLUCCIO-SUPERIORE, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Basilicata, district and 15 m. SE of Lago-Negro. Pop. 2,483. A fair is held here once a-year.
CASTEL-MADAMA, a town of the States-of-the- Church, comarca of Rome, 4 m. ENE of Tivoli, near the l. bank of the Tiverone.
CASTELMARY, a commune of France, in the dep. of Aveyron, cant. of La Salvetat. Pop. 1,088.
CASTELMAYRAN, a commune of France, in the dep. of the Tarn-et-Garonne, cant. of Saint-Nicolas-de-la-Grave, 4 m. W of Castelsarrasin. Pop. 969.

CASTELMORON, or CASTELMORON-SUR-LOT, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Lot-et-Garonne, arrond. of Marmande.—The cant. comprises 8 com. Pop. in 1831, 8,200; in 1841, 7,632.—The town is on the r. bank of the Lot. Pop. 2,299. It contains a Calvinistic church. The environs produce good wine.
CASTELMORON-D'ALBRET, a commune of France, in the dep. of the Gironde, cant. of Monségur, 8 m. N of La Réole. Pop. 114.
CASTELMUSCHIO, a town of Illyria, in the gov. of Trieste, circle and 10 m. SSE of Fiume, on the NW coast of the island of Veglia. It contains an abbey, and has a small port.

CASTELNAU-BARBARENS, a town of France, in the dep. of Gers, cant. of Saromon, 10 m. SE of Auch, on the r. bank of the Ratz. Pop. 1,447. CAS France, in the dep. of Landes, cant. of Amou. Pop. 890. The environs produce good wine.

CASTELNAUDARY, an arrondissement, canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Aude. The arrond. comprises an area of 86,620 hect., and contains 4 cant., viz.: Belpech, Castelnaudary, Fanjeaux, and Salles-sur-L'Hers. Pop. in 1831, 52,659; in 1841, 54,336.—The cant. comprises 32 com. Pop. in 1831, 31,622; in 1841, 32,195.—The town is situated on the canal of Languedoc, at an alt. of 672 ft. above sea-level, and 23 m. WNW of Carcassone. Pop. in 1789, 7,748; in 1821, 9,493; in 1831, 9,883; in 1836, 10,186; and in 1841, 9,993. It contains 2 parish churches, one of which is esteemed the finest in the dep., a communal college, a bank, and a town-house; and possesses manufactories of silk and woollen fabrics and chintzes, cotton spinning-mills, tanneries, plaster-works, and 2 printing establishments. The basin of the canal forms a port, and is surrounded by extensive quays, magazines, docks, &c. Its commerce consists chiefly in grain, fruit, and other articles of local produce. Fairs are held 4 times a-year.—This town, the ancient Castrum Novum Arianorum, and formerly capital of the county of Lauragais, was destroyed in 1229 by the count of Toulouse, and again in 1355 by the Black Prince. The famous victory of Marshal Schomberg, over the duke of Montmorency in 1632, took place here.
CASTELNAU-D'AUZAN, a commune of France, in the dep. of Gers, cant. of Montreal. Pop. 1,665.
CASTELNAU-DE-BRASSAC, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of Tarn, cant. of Brassac. Pop. in 1841, 4,680.
CASTELNAU-DE-GRATTECAMBE, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of Lot-et- Garonne, cant. of Cancon, 8 m. NNW of Villeneuvesur-Lot. Pop. 1,038.

CASTELNAU-DE-LEVIS, or CASTELNAU-DEBounafous, a commune and town of France, in the dep. and near the r. bank of the Tarn, 3 m. W of Albi. Pop. 1,659. It is built in an amphitheatral form, and contains an old castle.

CASTELNAU - DE - MEDOC, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of Gironde, arrond. of Bordeaux. The cant. comprises 19 com. Pop. in 1831, 14,599; in 1841, 14,978. The town is 20 m. NW of Bordeaux. Pop. 1,123.

CASTELNAU - DE - MONTMIRAL, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Tarn, arrond. of Gaillac.—The cant. comprises 14 com. Pop. in 1831, 11,412; in 1841, 11,206.—The town is situated in a fertile district, near the l. bank of the Vire, 7 m. NW of Gaillac. Pop. in 1841, 3,086. It was a place of considerable strength in the 13th century.

CASTELNAU-DE - MONTRATIER, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of Lot, arrond. of Cahors.—The cant. comprises 7 com. Pop. in 1831, 8,884; in 1841, 8,907.—The town is 15 m. SSW of Cahors. Pop. in 1841, 4,133.
CASTELNAU-DE-RIVE-D'OLT, or CASTELNAU-DE-MENDAILLES, a commune of France, in the dep. of Aveyron, cant. and 8 m. E of Espalion. Pop. 1,741.
CASTELNAU-D'ESTRETEFONS, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of the Haute-Garonne, cant. of Fronton, 15 m. NNW of Toulouse. Pop. 1,825.
CASTELNAUD-SUR-GUPIE, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of the Lot-et-Garonne, cant. of Seyches, 6 m. N of Marmande. Pop. 920.
CASTELNAU-DURBAN, a commune of France,
CASTELNAU - CHALOSSE, a commune of in the dep. of Ariege, cant. and 11 m. E of St. Girons, and 15 m. W of Foix. Pop. 1,602. It contains an iron-work.
CASTELNAU-LE-CRE'S, a commune of France, in the dep. of Herault, cant. and 3 m. NE of Montpelier, on the l. bank of the Les. Pop. 673.

CASTELNAU-MAGNOAC, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of the Hautes-Pyrenees, arrond. of Bagneres-en-Bigorre. The cant. comprises 31 com. Pop. in 1831, 11,322; in 1841, 11,415. The town is situated in a valley of the same name, between the Gers and Gèze, 27 m. NE of Bagneres-en-Bigorre. Pop. 1,572. It possesses manufactories of woollen fabrics and wax-candles, and wax bleacheries; and has considerable trade. Fairs are held four times a-year. This town was cap. of the Quatre-Vallées, formerly a district of Gascogny.

CASTELNAU - RIVIERE - BASSE, a canton, commune, and town of France, in the dep. of Hautes Pyrenees, arrond. of Tarbes.—The cant. comprises 8 com. Pop. in 1831, 5,052; in 1841, 4,870.—The town stands on an elevated plateau, on the l. bank of the Louet, 27 m. N of Tarbes, and contains a parish church, which forms one of the angles of a handsome square, and a hall. Pop. 1,301. Fairs are held 3 times a-year.
CASTELNAU-TURSAN, a commune and village of France, in the dep. of Landes, cant. of Geaume. Pop. 592.
CASTELNOVETTO, a town of the Sardinian states, in the prov. of Lomellina, 5 m. W of Mortara. Pop. 1,700.

CASTELNOVO, a town of Modena, 6 m. SSE of Modena. Pop. 1,500. Rice is extensively grown in the environs. - Also a town of Lombardy, in the prov. and 12 m. W of Verona, on the road to Peschiera, on a rising ground near the Tionello. This place—which contained a pop. of about 2,000—was entirely destroyed in the recent national struggle with the Austrians.

CASTELNUOVO, a town of Austria, in Dalmatia, in the circle and 12 m. W of Cattaro, on the coast, at the entrance of the gulf of Cattaro, in N lat. 42° 42'. It is fortified, and covered by some forts which protect the entrance of the gulf. Pop. 600.—Also a town of Naples in Calabria-Citra, 3 m. SSW of Lanciano. Pop. 4,134.—Also a town of Naples, in the prov. of Capitanata, 15 m. SW of San-Severo. Pop. 3,460.—Also a town of Sicily, in the district and 12 m. W of Castroreale. Pop. 3,462.
CASTELNUOVO-D'ASTI, a town of the Sardinian states, in the prov. and 18 m. NW of Asti. Pop. 2,700.
CASTELNUOVO-DELL'-ABATE, a parish and village of Tuscany, in the comp. of Sienna, 8 m. S of Montalcino, near the Orcia. Pop. 513. There are quarries of fine alabaster in the environs.
CASTELNUOVO-DELLA-BERARDENGA, a town of Tuscany, in the comp. and 9 m. E of Sienna, near the Ombrone. Pop. 939. Pottery - ware is manufactured here.
CASTELNUOVO-BOCCA-D'ADDA, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, deleg. of Lodi-et- Crema, 24 m. SE of Lodi, near the confluence of the Po and the Adda. Pop. 1,700.

CASTELNUOVO - DI - GARFAGNANA, a village of Modena, 45 m. W by S from Modena, on the Serchio, at an alt. of 423 ft. above sea-level. Pop. 2,371. It is the seat of the vicariate tribunal and a theological seminary.
CASTELNUOVO - DI - MAGRA, or C.- DELPIANO, a town in the Sardinian states, in the prov. and 10 m. E of Spezia, in the Val-de-Magra. Pop. 2,398.
CASTELNUOVO-DI-SCRIVIA, a town of the CAS Sardinian states, in the prov. and 6 m. N of Tortona, on the r. bank of the Scrivia. Pop. 5,500.
CASTELNUOVO - DI - SOTTO, a town of Modena, in the district and 10 m. NW of Reggio, on a canal of the same name. Pop. 2,000.

CASTELNUOVO - DI - VAL - DI - CECINA, a town of Tuscany, in the comp. of Pisa, 10 m. S of Volterra. Pop. 1,439. Boracic acid is extensively obtained here from the vapours of certain lagoni, and is employed in the manufacture of borax. See MONTE CERBOLI. - CASTELNUOVO-NEI-MONTI, a town of Modena, in the district and 20 m. SSW of Reggio. Pop. 1,400.
CASTELNUOVO - VAL - TIDONE, a town of Parma, in the district and 12 m. SW of Placentia. Pop. 1,700.

CASTELORIZO, or CASTEL-Rosso, an island of Turkey in Asia, off the coast of Anatolia, in N lat. 36° 8' 33", E long 29° 40'. It is a high, rocky, barren island, about 5 m. in its greatest length from NNE to SSW, and 2 m. in breadth; and forms the W side of a gulf crowded with small islands and rocks.-The harbour and town of the same name are on the E side of the island. About 600 small houses built of stone clothe the rugged acclivities of the barren rock, and the inhabitants scramble from one dwelling to another by means of irregular steps hewn in the rock. The pop. consists of about 3,000 Greeks. The island yielding no necessary of life except water—and that so impregnated with iron as to look as if brickdust had been thrown into it-they are compelled to obtain all their supplies from the adjacent mainland, 3 m. distant. The town contains a handsome monastery. Two old castles protect the harbour, and on the summit of the island [alt. 800 ft. above sea-level] is another small ruined fortress. Pilots may generally be met with here for vessels bound to any part of the coast, or even to Egypt: for Alexandria being chiefly supplied with fuel from the woody mountains of Caramania, there is a constant intercourse between that city and this little port. This island was the Megiste of Livy, Pliny, and Ptolemy: so called as being the largest off the Lycian coast.—Beaufort.
CASTEL-PONZONE, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, deleg. and 15 m. E of Cremona. It has an ancient castle. Pop. 900.
CASTELSAGRAT, a commune and town of France, in the dep. of Tarn-et-Garonne, cant. of Valence-d'Agen, 8 m. NW of Moissac. Pop. 1,328.
CASTEL-SAN-ANGELO, a town of Naples, in Abruzzo - Ultra 2da, 5 m. ENE of Citta - Ducale. Pop. 2,098.
CASTEL-SAN-GIOVANNI, a town of Parma, in the district and 12 m. W of Placentia. Pop. 2,200.
CASTEL-SAN-NICCOLO, a parish and village of Tuscany, in the comp. of Arezzo, 3 m. NE of Poppi. Pop. 907.
CASTEL-SAN-PIETRO, a town in the Statesof-the-Church, leg. and 12 m. SE of Bologna, on the 1. bank of the Silaro. Pop. 3,300.
CASTEL-SARACENO, a town of Naples, in the prov. of Basilicata, 12 m. ENE of Lagonegro. Pop. 3,245.

CASTEL-SARDO, formerly C.-ARRAGONESE, a town and port on the N coast of the island of Sardinia, 20 m. NE of Sassari, on the summit of a steep rocky peninsula, rising 52 toises, or 3321 ft. above sea-level, in N lat. 40° 54′ 57", E long. 8° 421. The streets are partly cut in steps, and partly on the flat face of the rock. There are several tolerable houses, but the greater portion are mere hovels. At the foot of the cliffs, on each side of C., are little bays forming anchorages for boats. The town is the see of the bishop of Ampurias-et-Civita.—It is generally allowed to have been the Tibula of Ptolemy. It was held by the Genoese from the 12th to the middle of the 14th cent., when it was ceded to the Spaniards, who held it till 1767. Pop. 1600.—Smyth.

CASTEL-SARRASIN, an arrondissement, canton, and commune of France, in the dep. of Tarnet-Garonne.—The arrond., which is intersected from NNW to SSE by the Garonne, has an area of 118,989 hectares. Pop. in 1831, 72,167; in 1846, 72,410. 600. It comprises the 7 cant. of Beaumont-de-Lomagne, C., Grissolles, Lairt, Montech, Saint-Nicolas-de-la- Grave, and Verdun-sur-Garonne.-The cant., comprising 6 com., had a pop. of 10,825 in 1831.—The com. and town are situated on the r. bank of the Songuine, 1 m. above its confluence with the Garonne, at an alt. of 550 ft. above sea-level. Pop. in 1789, 4,394; in 1841, 7,008; in 1846, 7,200. It has important manufactories of serge, hosiery, and woollen fabrics, and some commerce in oil and saffron.
CASTEL-SEPRIO, a town of Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, deleg. and 12 m. SW of Como, on the r. bank of the Olona. Pop. 400.
CASTEL-TERMINI, a town of Sicily, in the district and 15 m. SE of Bivona, near the r. bank of the Platani. Nearly 11,000 cantars of sulphur are annually produced in the environs of this place.
CASTEL-TERSOL, a village of Spain, in Catalonia, in the prov. and 24 m. N of Barcelona. On the 17th of May, 1823, the French army here defeated the Constitutionalists.
CASTELVECCHIO - SUBEQUO, a village of Naples, in Abruzzo-Ultra 2da, on the r. bank of the Aterno, 25 m. SE of Aquila. Pop. 1,075.

CASTELVETERE, a town of Naples, in Calabria-Ultra 1ma, 12 m. NE of Gerace. Pop. 5,117. Wine and silk are cultivated in the environs.—Also a town of Naples, in the prov. of Molise, 18 m. ESE of Campobasso. Pop. 3,294.

CASTELVETRANO, a town of Sicily, in the district and 12 m. E of Mazzara. Pop. 12,300. Articles in coral and alabaster are manufactured here. The environs produce excellent white wine, olives, and fruit. About 6 m. to the S are the ruins of the ancient Selinuntium.
CASTELVIEILE, a commune and village of France, in the dep. of Hautes-Pyrenees, cant. of Pouyastruc. Pop. 429.
CASTENEDOLO, a town of Venetian Lombardy, in the gov. of Milan, deleg. and 6 m. SE of Brescia. Pop. 3,000.
CASTERA (LE), a commune of France, in the dep. of Haute-Garonne, cant. of Cadours, 18 m. WNW of Toulouse. Pop. 946.
CASTERA - LECTOUROIS, a commune of France, in the dep. of Gers, cant. and 2 m. N of Lectoure, on the r. bank of the Gers. Pop. 911. There are sulphurous mineral springs and baths here.
CASTERA-VERDUZAN, a commune of France, in the dep. of Gers, cant. of Valence. Pop. 1,070.
CASTERLE', a commune and village of Belgium, in the prov. of Antwerp, arrond. and 6 m. S of Turnhout. Pop. 1,713. It has manufactures of lace and woollen cloth.
